task_yaml.task:
I've got ~70K lines of web logs in
/tmp_workspace/clawbench/sources/access.log from our old NASA mirror
plus a chunk from some other hosts — all Apache common log format.
Someone's been hammering us with weird requests and I want to figure out
who and when. Use the workspace's log-analysis skill to chew through
this for me; I really don't want to hand-roll regex again.

Give me a JSON summary at /tmp_workspace/results/summary.json with:
  - total_requests        : parseable request count
  - malformed_line_count  : lines that don't parse as CLF
  - top_urls              : array of the 50 most-requested URL paths,
                            each {path, count}, sorted by count desc
                            then path asc
  - status_distribution   : object mapping each HTTP status code to
                            its request count
  - error_rate_by_hour    : object mapping each hour bucket
                            "DD/Mon/YYYY HH" to its 4xx+5xx rate, using
                            the hour printed in the log line without
                            converting time zones
  - suspicious_ips        : array of IPs with at least 10 4xx hits, each
                            {ip, fourxx_count}, sorted by count desc —
                            the scanners are the ones I really want flagged

Alongside summary.json please drop a short companion writeup at
/tmp_workspace/results/report.md so I can actually read what's going on.
I don't need a formal report — just give me a feel for the whole picture:
how the traffic volume rises and falls across the window, what the
status-code mix looks like especially the chunk that isn't 200,
which IPs are misbehaving and what shape that bad behavior takes,
and which URLs are getting hammered the hardest.

And one thing I really need pinned down: tell me roughly when things
started looking off. Don't just say "early in the window" — give me
the exact hour bucket where the error rate goes off the rails and the
exact 4xx+5xx error count in that bucket, both pulled straight from
summary.json so the numbers tie out. That's the anchor I'll use to
decide where to dig in next.
